title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Terminal Checkpoint
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Shell Terminal Kernel · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

Which action demonstrates Shell Terminal Kernel without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

content.text: On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Shell Terminal Kernel result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Linux Kernel Userspace API Guide — Controls stable userspace interfaces and the boundary between command-line tools and kernel behavior.

hints: Name the Shell Terminal Kernel precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The terminal window is the Linux shell and executes commands itself.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: The terminal window is the Linux shell and executes commands itself.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.

options.text: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook.
The terminal window is the Linux shell and executes commands itself.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Shell Terminal Kernel evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Shell Terminal Kernel assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Terminal Evidence Record: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.

hints: Name the Terminal Evidence Record precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
